calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Oct-16-2020 Vacated

Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Award Of Attorneys Fees Costs And Litigation Expenses
Off Calendar Jul-22-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Joan Herrington for Plaintiff, Kellan Dunn.
Steven Derby for Plaintiff.
Kate G. Kimberlin (415.554.3847) Deputy City Attorney for Defendant, City and County of San Francisco.
Jessica Pliner for Defendants, Corp. of Fine Arts Museum, et al.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF KELLAN DUNN's Motion For An Award Of Attorneys Fees Costs And Litigation Expenses:
Plaintiff Kellan Dunn's motion for an award of attorneys' fees, costs, and litigation expenses is granted in the total amount of $116,394.04 in attorneys' fees and $7,426.51 in costs. This award represents a 0.7 negative multiplier on counsel's lodestar of $143,042.50 (Ms. Herrington) and $23,235.00 (Derby, McGuinness & Goldsmith, LLP). The Court finds that the amount awarded represents a reasonable amount in light of all of the facts and circumstances. The case did not require any briefing or hearing on pleading motions or motions for summary judgment/adjudication, involved very little discovery, three contested discovery motions (all brought by defendants and granted against plaintiff), and a failed mediation. Further, plaintiff's failure to comply with the Court's first discovery order granting defendants' motion to compel resulted in the Court awarding both monetary and evidentiary sanctions against plaintiff. The case resolved for a modest settlement amount ($60,000) when plaintiff accepted defendants' first section 998 offer before propounding any written discovery or taking any depositions.

Plaintiff's lodestar includes time spent preparing deficient discovery responses and unsuccessfully opposing defendants' motions to compel and for sanctions without substantial justification, and also reflects significant inefficiency and duplication to file and prosecute a relatively simple case. Based on the record, the Court finds that not all of the time for which plaintiff seeks compensation was necessary and reasonably incurred, and that a substantial reduction of plaintiff's lodestar therefore is appropriate. (See, e.g., Warren v. Kia Motors America, Inc. (2018) 30 Cal.App.5th 24, 37, 40-41 [trial court may apply a negative multiplier to adjust the lodestar figure to arrive at a reasonable fee if it "clearly explain[s] its case-specific reasons for the percentage reduction," which may properly include excessive time spent on case by multiple attorneys]; Save Our Uniquely Rural Community Environment v. County of San Bernadino (2015) 235 Cal.App.4th 1179, 1188 [trial court did not abuse its discretion in reducing lodestar by 80 percent].)

Counsel for Plaintiff to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.
Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
